title: An Account of al-Muwallad's Expedition
description: Muhammad al-Muwallad reached the area and was quartered in alUbullah 382 Burayh arrived and established himself in al-Ba^rah. A large number of Bagrans who had previously fled the city gathered (x858) around Burayh. When Yahya (b. Muhammad) withdrew from the city, he set up camp on the Nahr al-Ghutha. Muhammad b. al-Hasan, reporting from Shibl, said that, when Muhammad al-Muwallad arrived, the abominable one sent Yahya instructions to proceed to the Nahr Awwa. He arrived there with his army and set about engaging al-Muwallad in battle for ten days.

sources.citation: al-Tabari, The History of al-Tabari (SUNY), vol. 36
